payload.message: Check if a new version of OpenClaw is available. Run `openclaw --version` or equivalent to get the current installed version, then check the OpenClaw GitHub releases page (https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases) or npm registry for the latest version. Compare the two. If a newer version is available, send a Telegram message to Solo (channel: telegram) with: the current version, the new version number, and a brief summary of what changed based on the release notes. If already up to date, do nothing and stay silent (NO_REPLY).

Deliver a daily AI news briefing for Han. Search for the latest AI news from today, including sources like Latent.Space, SemiAnalysis, GitHub (trending/releases), The Verge, The Information, and deeplearning.ai (Data Points tag at https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/tag/data-points/). Exclude: Hacker News, Discord. Format it as a structured briefing with: 1) Top 5-7 stories with headlines, brief summaries, SOURCE URLS, and direct links 2) A 'Signal Watch' section highlighting the most important or unexpected development with URLs 3) A brief overall assessment of the day's news. Make it concise but substantive—focus on news that matters to AI/ML practitioners and governance professionals. Include the sources explicitly.

**CRITICAL: For EVERY story, include the direct URL to the original article or source.** Format as: **[Headline](URL)** so Han can click through immediately if he wants more details. Don't skip URLs—they're essential.

After creating the briefing, output it in plain text format. Then:
1. Send the briefing to Telegram (8128617103) using the message tool with action=send.
2. Also send the same briefing via email using exec to run: node -e "const{AgentMailClient}=require('/home/node/workspace/skills/agentmail/node_modules/agentmail');const c=new AgentMailClient({apiKey:process.env.AGENTMAIL_API_KEY});c.inboxes.messages.send('artooopenclaw@agentmail.to',{to:['han.yang123@yahoo.com'],subject:'🤖 AI Daily Briefing - [TODAY_DATE]',text:BRIEFING_TEXT_HERE}).then(r=>console.log('Email sent:',r.messageId)).catch(e=>console.error('Email error:',e.message));"

## Step 1 — Read the existing knowledge base

Read the file at /home/node/workspace/AGENT_INFRA.md. Extract all existing entry titles and URLs so you know what has already been documented. If the file does not exist, treat the knowledge base as empty.

## Step 2 — Research

Search for EMERGING and NOVEL services, APIs, and infrastructure designed specifically for AI agents. Focus on: 1) Software-for-Agent-as-a-Service (SfAaS) platforms and tools, 2) New or emerging agent-native tooling (not established platforms like LangChain/AutoGen), 3) Agent orchestration systems that solve novel problems, 4) Model-to-model communication APIs, 5) Agent monitoring, observability, and debugging infrastructure, 6) Agent financial/economic systems (e.g., agent-to-agent payments, micropayments for model calls), 7) Emerging agent governance and safety infrastructure, 8) Infrastructure for multi-agent coordination at scale. Also SYNTHESIZE gaps: What infrastructure do agents need that doesn't exist yet?

Prioritize emerging, cutting-edge, or novel approaches. Skip obvious/well-known products (LangChain, AutoGen, etc.). Look for: GitHub trending (novel agent tools), Product Hunt (emerging launches), ArXiv papers on agent infrastructure, X/Twitter discussions from AI researchers, newsletters focused on agent development.

**CRITICAL: For EVERY discovery, include the direct URL** (product page, GitHub repo, announcement link). Format as **[Name](URL)**.

## Step 3 — Deduplicate

Compare your findings against the existing entries in AGENT_INFRA.md. A finding is a DUPLICATE if the same product/service/paper is already in the knowledge base (match by URL or title similarity). Skip all duplicates. Only keep findings that are genuinely new.

## Step 4 — Update AGENT_INFRA.md

For each NEW finding, append an entry to /home/node/workspace/AGENT_INFRA.md between the <!-- ENTRIES_START --> and <!-- ENTRIES_END --> markers, in this exact format:

```
### [DATE] [CATEGORY] Title
**URL:** url-here
**Why it matters:** one or two sentences on what problem it solves and why it is significant for agent infrastructure.

---
```

Where DATE is today's date (YYYY-MM-DD), CATEGORY is one of: [PLATFORM] [API] [ORCHESTRATION] [OBSERVABILITY] [PAYMENTS] [GOVERNANCE] [RESEARCH] [GAP].

Also update the last line "Total entries: N" at the bottom of the file with the new total count.

If there are NO new findings (all duplicates), skip the file update entirely.

## Step 5 — Brief Solo

Create a briefing containing ONLY the new findings from today (not duplicates). Include:
1) New discoveries with name, URL, category, and why it matters
2) A 'SYNTHESIZED NEEDS' section on gaps and patterns
3) A note on how many items are now in the knowledge base total

If there are no new findings, send a short message: 'Agent Infra scan complete — no new entries today. Knowledge base holds [N] entries.'

Then:
1. Send the briefing to Telegram (8128617103) using the message tool with action=send.
2. Also send the same briefing via email to han.yang123@yahoo.com using exec: node -e "const{AgentMailClient}=require('/home/node/workspace/skills/agentmail/node_modules/agentmail');const c=new AgentMailClient({apiKey:process.env.AGENTMAIL_API_KEY});c.inboxes.messages.send('artooopenclaw@agentmail.to',{to:['han.yang123@yahoo.com'],subject:'🔮 Agent Needs Briefing - [TODAY_DATE]',text:BRIEFING_TEXT_HERE}).then(r=>console.log('Email sent:',r.messageId)).catch(e=>console.error('Email error:',e.message));"

## Weekly Agent Infrastructure Knowledge Base Review

Read the file at /home/node/workspace/AGENT_INFRA.md.

Perform the following review tasks:

1) **Consolidate duplicates** — identify any entries covering the same product or service (possibly added on different days). Merge them into a single entry keeping the most recent date and the most complete description.

2) **Flag stale entries** — entries older than 60 days that have been superseded by a newer version of the same product should be marked with a `[STALE]` tag at the start of their title.

3) **Identify themes** — based on all entries, write a short 'Patterns & Trends' section at the top of the knowledge base (below the header, above ENTRIES_START) summarizing: what categories are most represented, what gaps keep appearing, and what direction agent infrastructure seems to be heading. Replace any existing Patterns & Trends section.

4) **Update the metadata** at the bottom: set 'Last reviewed' to today's date and update 'Total entries' count.

5) **Write the updated file** back to /home/node/workspace/AGENT_INFRA.md.

6) **Send a brief summary to Solo** via Telegram (8128617103): how many entries reviewed, how many merged, how many flagged stale, and 2-3 sentences on the key trends you observed.
